title:
Cybersecurity recruitment challenges:
Share of companies challenged by rapidly changing cybersecurity technologies
description: Identifies the key obstacles organisations face when trying to recruit staff with the appropriate cybersecurity skills. This indicator helps highlight specific barriers in the cybersecurity talent acquisition process.

title:
Cybersecurity recruitment challenges:
Share of companies citing lack of applicants for cybersecurity roles
description: Identifies the key obstacles organisations face when trying to recruit staff with the appropriate cybersecurity skills. This indicator helps highlight specific barriers in the cybersecurity talent acquisition process.

Cybersecurity recruitment challenges:

Cybersecurity recruitment challenges:

title:
Cybersecurity skills gap in hiring:
Share of enterprises having no difficulties in hiring staff with appropriate cybersecurity skills
description: Measures the level of challenge companies face in finding and hiring staff with the appropriate cybersecurity skills.

title:
Cybersecurity skills gap in hiring:
Share of enterprises having some difficulties in hiring staff with appropriate cybersecurity skills
description: Measures the level of challenge companies face in finding and hiring staff with the appropriate cybersecurity skills.

title:
Cybersecurity talent demand:
Share of companies reporting at least one Cybersecurity position (FTE) needing to be filled
description: Helps to understand the scale of the cybersecurity staffing needs.

title:
Cybersecurity workforce within companies:
Share of companies employing at least one Cybersecurity professionals
description: Measures the share of companies that employ at least one employee within a company who holds positions or performs roles directly related to cybersecurity, providing insight into the organisation's capacity to defend against cyber threats.

title:
Most critical Cybersecurity skills:
Share of companies prioritising social engineering as key skill
description: Helps to assess the skills gap in the cybersecurity workforce and prioritise efforts in developing, recruiting, or training the necessary skills to address the most important cybersecurity challenges they face.

title:
Pathways to cybersecurity employment:
Share of companies integrating Cybersecurity roles into existing non-Cybersecurity positions
description: Measures how employees currently involved in cybersecurity roles within a company entered their positions. It helps identify the most common routes into cybersecurity roles, offering insights into workforce development trends and talent acquisition strategies.

title: Pathways to cybersecurity employment: Share of companies recruited employees from a previous role in cyber security
description: Measures how employees currently involved in cybersecurity roles within a company entered their positions. It helps identify the most common routes into cybersecurity roles, offering insights into workforce development trends and talent acquisition strategies.

title: Pathways to cybersecurity employment: Share of companies recruited employees from non-cybersecurity roles
description: Measures how employees currently involved in cybersecurity roles within a company entered their positions. It helps identify the most common routes into cybersecurity roles, offering insights into workforce development trends and talent acquisition strategies.

title: Pathways to cybersecurity employment: Share of companies recruiting employees as career starters (e.g., Graduates)
description: Measures how employees currently involved in cybersecurity roles within a company entered their positions. It helps identify the most common routes into cybersecurity roles, offering insights into workforce development trends and talent acquisition strategies.

title: Share of budgets granted for Cybersecurity projects across participating countries
description: Measures the proportion of the total allocated budget for projects distributed among different participating countries. Helps in assessing how project funds are distributed geographically.

title: Share of budgets granted for Cybersecurity projects per type of the participant: Share of budgets granted for cybersecurity projects to NGOs
description: Measures the proportion of total funding allocated to different types of participants involved in cybersecurity projects. It breaks down the distribution of cybersecurity project budgets across various participant categories, such as NGOs, private companies, public entities, research institutions, SMEs, and universities. Provides a clear view of how cybersecurity project funding is distributed among different organisations.

title: Share of Cybersecurity projects across participating countries
description: Measures the distribution of cybersecurity projects among participating countries.

title: Share of Cybersecurity projects budget per million people
description: Measures how much of the total project budget is allocated per one million inhabitants in each country. This indicator helps compare the relative investment or funding distribution across countries, considering population differences.

description: Measures the proportion of different participant groups (such as NGOs, private companies, public entities, research institutions, SMEs, and universities) that receive financial support or grants for cybersecurity-related projects.

description: Measures the proportion of different participant groups (such as NGOs, private companies, public entities, research institutions, SMEs, and universities) that receive financial support or grants for cybersecurity-related projects.

title: Share of tender budget per million people
description: Measures how much of the total tender budget is allocated per one million inhabitants in each country. This indicator helps compare the relative investment or funding distribution across countries, considering population differences.

title: Share of tender budgets across participating countries
description: Measures the proportion of the total allocated budget for tenders distributed among different participating countries. Helps in assessing how tender funds are distributed geographically.

title: Share of tenders per country
description: Measures the proportion of total tenders (e.g., procurement opportunities, bids, or contracts) that are associated with each country. It provides insight into the level of tender activity in each country.

title:
Top in-demand Cybersecurity positions:

description: Helps to determine which cybersecurity roles are essential for safeguarding their systems and data. The positions can be prioritised in terms of hiring, training, and investment.
